{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48702", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "## Description

The `Package.Unmarshal()` function in `pkg/types/alpine/apk.go` decompresses the signature and control gzip members of an APK file into in-memory buffers without bounding the total decompressed size. The existing `max_apk_metadata_size` check (default 1MB) is only applied to individual tar entry header sizes after decompression completes, so it does not prevent a decompression bomb from consuming unbounded heap memory.

An attacker can craft a gzip stream that compresses at a ~1000:1 ratio (e.g., 2MB compressed zeros → 2GB decompressed). When submitted as spec.package.content in an Alpine `ProposedEntry`, the server decompresses the full payload into memory during request processing, triggering a fatal Go runtime out-of-memory error or OS OOM-kill that cannot be caught by the server's recover() middleware.

This is reachable via two unauthenticated endpoints:
- POST /api/v1/log/entries (createLogEntry)
- POST /api/v1/log/entries/retrieve (searchLogQuery)

Both invoke `V001Entry.Canonicalize()` → `fetchExternalEntities()` → `apk.Unmarshal(packageData)`, which performs the unbounded decompression.

## Workarounds

There is no effective workaround. Setting `max_request_body_size` reduces but does not eliminate exposure due to the ~1000:1 compression ratio (a 1MB body limit still allows ~1GB heap allocation). Setting `max_apk_metadata_size` has no effect on this vulnerability since the check is applied after decompression.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-47q9-m4ww-924m", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-25T21:33:36Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-47q9-m4ww-924m", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-47q9-m4ww-924m"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48702"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-25T21:33:36Z", "references": ["https://github.com/sigstore/rekor/security/advisories/GHSA-47q9-m4ww-924m", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-47q9-m4ww-924m"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/sigstore/rekor/security-advisories/GHSA-47q9-m4ww-924m", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/sigstore/rekor", "summary": "Rekor has an OOM Condition due to Unbounded gzip Decompression in Alpine APK Parsing Logic", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-25T21:33:39Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-47q9-m4ww-924m", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.5.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/sigstore/rekor"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 0.3.0, < 1.5.2"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Rekor has an OOM Condition due to Unbounded gzip Decompression in Alpine APK Parsing Logic
